title: viola pedatifida subsp. brittoniana
intro: Viola pedatifida subsp. brittoniana, also known as Britton's viola, is a plant species that belongs to the viola genus, characterized by its unique, divided leaves and delicate purple flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Britton's viola include its potential to alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression, as well as its ability to aid in the healing of wounds and reduce inflammation. Britton's viola is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be found in dried form for use in herbal remedies.
